data.content: I can see you have one failing test — `ExpressDelivery` expects `15.00m` but the method returns `10.00m`. Let me start `dotnet watch test` so it re-runs on every file save:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload on their test project. Let me start it up for them. This is an MSTest project targeting .NET 8.0.

I can see there's a failing test: `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects `15.00m` but `CalculateShipping("express", 1.0)` returns `10.00m`.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so they can iterate.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-775a028fd86e43078137b4b680a07199 && dotnet watch test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.reasoningText: The `--no-pager` flag got passed through to MSBuild which doesn't understand it. Let me restart without that flag.

data.content: `dotnet watch test` is running and watching for changes. There's a build error — missing `using System;`. Let me fix that:
data.reasoningText: There's a build error: `ArgumentException` needs `using System;` or the full qualifier. Let me fix that so the project builds, and the watch will automatically re-run.
